_comment: P4-WRITE demo: a single `crenel expose <svc> --auth authelia` lands the coordinated entries across the FRONT (vps) + DOWNSTREAM (home) edges + DNS as ONE all-or-nothing, read-back-verified transaction — applied downstream -> front -> public-DNS (announce the name to the world LAST). The front FORWARDS to downstream_address (no auth); auth attaches at the HOME edge that SERVES the host. Both edges are deny-only in-process fakes (reset per CLI run), so the demo is a single expose invocation showing the changeset + the per-edge/per-DNS read-back. Try: crenel expose books --auth authelia -config examples/settings-chain-write.json -yes  (and: drop --auth to see the chain-wide public-without-auth guardrail refuse).
